task_completion.description: OBS Studio is opened and an Image source is configured in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OBS Studio window is visible with an 'Image' source present in the Sources panel.

task_completion.description: The first QR code (equip_1.png) is decoded using bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OBS Studio displays equip_1.png, and bcWebCam shows the decoded URL, or a text editor is open with the first decoded URL pasted.

task_completion.description: The second QR code (equip_2.png) is decoded using bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OBS Studio displays equip_2.png, and bcWebCam shows the decoded URL, or a text editor is open with two decoded URLs pasted.

task_completion.description: The third QR code (equip_3.png) is decoded using bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OBS Studio displays equip_3.png, and bcWebCam shows the decoded URL, or a text editor is open with three decoded URLs pasted.

task_completion.description: The extracted URLs are saved into a single text file at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) contains three URLs on separate lines, and the window title or a 'Save As' dialog indicates the file is named 'equipment_ids.txt' and saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\'.

integrity.description: The agent used OBS Studio and bcWebCam for decoding.
integrity.visual_evidence: OBS Studio and bcWebCam windows are actively used in the screenshots. There should be no web browsers open to online QR code decoders.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to decode the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or command prompts are visible executing Python scripts, zbarimg, or other command-line decoding tools.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor to save the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor like Notepad is used to paste the URLs and save the file, rather than terminal commands (like echo or Out-File) being used to create the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is C:\Users\Docker\Documents\equipment_ids.txt.
